entries.label: Basic trait
entries.note: Declare a trait with required method signatures

entries.label: Default implementation
entries.note: Provide a body; implementors can override

entries.label: Multiple methods
entries.note: Defaults can call other trait methods

entries.label: Associated types
entries.note: Placeholder type defined by implementor

entries.label: Associated constants
entries.note: Constants that implementors must define

entries.label: Implement for struct
entries.note: impl TraitName for TypeName { ... }

entries.label: Implement for enum
entries.note: Traits can be implemented for enums too

entries.label: Orphan rule
entries.note: Either the trait or the type must be local to your crate

entries.label: Blanket implementation
entries.note: Implement a trait for all types that satisfy a bound

entries.label: Bound syntax
entries.note: T must implement Display

entries.label: impl Trait syntax
entries.note: Sugar for simple trait bounds in function parameters

entries.label: Multiple bounds
entries.note: Use + to require multiple traits

entries.label: Where clause
entries.code:
fn complex<T, U>(t: &T, u: &U) -> String
where
    T: Display + Clone,
    U: Debug + PartialOrd,
{
    format!("{t}: {u:?}")
}
entries.note: Cleaner syntax for complex bounds

entries.label: Return impl Trait
entries.note: Return an opaque type that implements a trait

entries.label: Trait bound on struct
entries.note: Generic structs can have trait bounds too

entries.label: Trait object
entries.note: Runtime dispatch via vtable; use when type is not known at compile time

entries.label: Boxed trait object
entries.note: Heap-allocated trait object; needed for returning different concrete types

entries.label: Vec of trait objects
entries.note: Heterogeneous collection of different types

entries.label: dyn vs impl
entries.note: impl Trait = compile-time; dyn Trait = runtime. Choose based on needs

entries.label: Object safety
entries.note: Only object-safe traits can be used as dyn Trait

entries.label: Supertrait requirement
entries.note: PrettyPrint requires implementors to also implement Display

entries.label: Multiple supertraits
entries.note: Use + for multiple supertrait requirements

entries.label: Using supertraits
entries.note: Supertrait methods are automatically available

entries.label: Display
entries.note: User-facing output; used by {} in format strings

entries.label: Debug
entries.note: Developer-facing output; used by {:?} and {:#?} (pretty)

entries.label: From / Into
entries.note: Implement From; you get Into for free

entries.label: TryFrom / TryInto
entries.note: Fallible conversion; returns Result

entries.label: AsRef / AsMut
entries.note: Cheap reference-to-reference conversions

entries.label: ToString / FromStr
entries.note: Implement Display to get ToString for free; FromStr for parsing

entries.label: PartialEq / Eq
entries.note: PartialEq for == and !=; Eq adds reflexivity guarantee (a == a)

entries.label: PartialOrd / Ord
entries.note: PartialOrd for < > <= >=; Ord for total ordering (required for sort)

entries.label: Hash
entries.note: Required for HashMap/HashSet keys; derive with Eq

entries.label: Default
entries.note: Default values: 0 for numbers, false for bool, empty for String/Vec

entries.label: Iterator
entries.note: Implement next() to create a custom iterator

entries.label: IntoIterator
entries.note: Allows your type to be used in for loops

entries.label: Clone
entries.note: Explicit deep copy; may be expensive for heap data

entries.label: Drop
entries.note: Custom cleanup when value goes out of scope; cannot be called explicitly (use std::mem::drop)
